zero_slop_policy.statement: NO AI SLOP ALLOWED AT ALL - ZERO TOLERANCE TO SLOP AND HALLUCINATIONS
zero_slop_policy.strict_compliance: Mandatory. Every claim must be project-specific, evidence-backed, trace-linked, reviewer-challenged, and free of unsupported invention.
zero_slop_policy.missing_evidence_rule: If evidence is missing, mark it as an assumption, risk, open question, or failure.

vision.statement: Deliver a walkable, Hawkeye-auditable Demo MVP of an estate-and-tax-planning platform clone (working title 'EstateCompass') that exercises Auth+Tenant+Identity, Households+People+Entities, Documents+WORM Audit, an Ester-substitute extractor, federal estate-tax baseline calc under OBBBA $15M permanent for ONE scenario, GRAT-vs-do-nothing scenario for $20M household, a minimal advisor walkable UI, and 2 integration adapter mocks (Plaid-shape + Schwab-shape) — with deterministic-calc separation, multi-tenant isolation, and synthetic data only.

capabilities.name: Multi-Tenant Identity, RBAC, and Audit-Log Isolation
capabilities.statement: Multi-tenant identity service with SSO-stub login, MFA-stub, RBAC (advisor/paraplanner/admin), and Postgres Row-Level Security ensuring zero cross-tenant data visibility.

user_journeys.name: Advisor Login + Tenant-Scoped Dashboard
user_journeys.statement: Advisor authenticates via SSO-stub, completes MFA-stub, lands on a dashboard scoped to their tenant only.

leaves.statement: System provides /login route accepting OIDC-mock issuer credentials (email + password) and issues a session JWT with claims {user_id, tenant_id, roles[]}.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Given seed user sarah@horizon.test/seedpassword on tenant A, when POST /api/auth/login, then 200 with JWT containing tenant_id='A' and roles=['advisor']; given wrong password, then 401.

leaves.statement: System enforces TOTP-style MFA-stub on login: after password, prompt for 6-digit code; seed users have static seed code '123456'.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Given valid password, when login flow proceeds, then /api/auth/mfa-verify endpoint required; with wrong code 401; with seed code 200 + JWT.

leaves.statement: Login + MFA round-trip p95 latency <800ms on local Docker Compose.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: k6 or Vitest perf harness records p95<800ms over 100 sequential logins.

leaves.statement: JWT TTL is 8 hours; refresh requires re-MFA.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Decode token: exp - iat == 28800. After exp, refresh requires MFA re-prompt.

leaves.statement: System defines 3 roles for MVP: advisor, paraplanner, admin; each has specific scope set (advisor:read_household, advisor:write_extraction_review, paraplanner:read_household, admin:read_audit).
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Authorization middleware blocks paraplanner from PUT /households/:id with 403; admin can GET /audit-events with 200; advisor can GET /households with 200.

leaves.statement: MVP RBAC scope is intentionally reduced from PRD-0116's 40+ scopes to MVP minimum; full ABAC deferred (ASM-RBAC-001).
leaves.acceptance_criteria: ADR documents scope reduction with rollback plan; MVP audit confirms only 3 roles defined and tested.

leaves.statement: Every tenant-owned table has RLS enabled with policy USING (tenant_id = current_setting('app.current_tenant')::uuid); SET LOCAL app.current_tenant on each request transaction.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: psql query against households table without setting app.current_tenant returns 0 rows; with current_tenant=A returns tenant A rows only; attempting to INSERT a row with tenant_id != current_tenant raises policy violation.

leaves.statement: API middleware extracts tenant_id from JWT and SET LOCAL app.current_tenant before any DB query.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Forge request with tampered tenant_id in path returns 403; valid tenant_id auto-applied to all queries (verified by SQL trace).

leaves.statement: Negative tenant-isolation scenario test: tenant B credential querying GET /households returns only tenant B records (not tenant A).
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Tenant A seeded with 3 households, tenant B with 2; advisor B sees exactly 2; advisor A sees exactly 3; row count assertions hold.

leaves.statement: Security abuse test: IDOR attempt — request /households/:id where id belongs to other tenant returns 404 (not 403, to avoid existence leak).
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Forge request with foreign household UUID returns 404; audit-event logged with action='auth.idor_attempt'.

leaves.statement: Auth events emitted: auth.login_success, auth.login_failed, auth.mfa_failed, auth.idor_attempt, auth.role_denied — written to WORM audit log.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: After each login attempt, exactly one row in audit_events with action ∈ {auth.login_success, auth.login_failed} and actor_id, tenant_id, ts, request_id populated.

capabilities.name: Household, Person, Entity (Trust types) Domain Model
capabilities.statement: Domain model for Household → Persons → Entities (Trust placeholders: Revocable, GRAT, Dynasty) with relationships and basic CRUD via authenticated API.

user_journeys.name: Advisor browses household → person → entity hierarchy
user_journeys.statement: Advisor opens dashboard, selects household, drills into people and entities.

leaves.statement: GET /api/households returns array of households where tenant_id = current_tenant; each row includes id, name, totalAssetsUSD, headOfHouseholdName.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Tenant A request returns exactly 3 households (Ellison, Sharma, Kline); tenant B request returns exactly 2; response time p95 < 200ms.

leaves.statement: Household table schema: id (uuid PK), tenant_id (uuid FK), name (text), total_assets_usd (numeric), created_at, updated_at.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: SQL inspection shows table created with RLS policy and FK constraints; migration test green.

leaves.statement: GET /api/households/:id returns household + persons[] + entities[] + assets[]; tenant scoped; 404 if foreign tenant.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: GET /households/{Ellison-uuid} as tenant A returns {persons:[4], entities:[3], assets:[5+]}; same id as tenant B returns 404.

leaves.statement: Person table: id, tenant_id, household_id, first_name, last_name, date_of_birth, role (e.g., GRANTOR, SPOUSE, BENEFICIARY).
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Migration creates table with FK to households and RLS; seed inserts 4 persons for Ellison household.

leaves.statement: Entity table: id, tenant_id, household_id, type (enum REVOCABLE_TRUST, GRAT, DYNASTY_TRUST), name, allocated_exemption_usd, gst_inclusion_ratio (numeric 4dp).
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Type enum includes only the 3 MVP trust types; gst_inclusion_ratio constrained 0.0000-1.0000; sample row gstInclusionRatio:0 + allocatedExemption:13500000 (per PRD-0189).

leaves.statement: Asset table: id, tenant_id, household_id, owning_entity_id (nullable), titling (enum INDIVIDUAL, JTWROS, REVOCABLE_TRUST, GRAT, DYNASTY_TRUST), asset_class (enum CASH, PUBLIC_EQUITY, REAL_ESTATE, PRIVATE_EQUITY), value_usd (numeric).
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Sum of assets per household equals household.total_assets_usd; for Ellison: $20,000,000.

leaves.statement: Trust types restricted to {REVOCABLE_TRUST, GRAT, DYNASTY_TRUST} for MVP; SLAT/ILIT/CLAT/CRT/QPRT/IDGT explicitly excluded per CC-001 narrowing.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Schema constraint rejects insertion of any other type; ADR records out-of-scope trust types.

leaves.statement: Read events 'household.viewed' emitted on GET /households/:id with tenant_id, actor_id, household_id.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Audit table has matching row after each detail view.

leaves.statement: Seed script populates tenant A with 3 synthetic households (Ellison $20M, Sharma $5M, Kline $22M) and tenant B with 2 synthetic households; all data fabricated, no real PII.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Post `docker compose up`, SELECT count from households returns 5 total (3 tenant A + 2 tenant B); spot-check that no SSN-shaped or real-looking name appears in seed.

leaves.statement: Synthetic-data-only rule: no leaf may load real PII/PHI; brand_substitution_validator + synthetic_data_only_validator runtime gates apply.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Static check on seed files plus regex scan for SSN/credit-card patterns returns zero hits.

capabilities.name: Document Upload + WORM Audit + Encryption-at-Rest
capabilities.statement: Secure document upload flow with object-store storage, encryption-at-rest, and WORM (write-once-read-many) audit log capturing immutable state-change events.

user_journeys.name: Advisor uploads a document and audit captures the event
user_journeys.statement: Advisor uploads sample-will.pdf via drag-drop on household detail; system stores PDF, creates document row, writes WORM audit row.

leaves.statement: POST /api/households/:id/documents accepts multipart PDF up to 25MB; stores raw bytes in object store; creates documents row with sha256_hash, raw_storage_key, status='UPLOADED'.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Curl-equivalent multipart POST returns 201 with documentId; SQL shows documents row with sha256 matching file; object-store key exists.

leaves.statement: documents table: id, tenant_id, household_id, document_type (e.g., REVOCABLE_TRUST), raw_storage_key, sha256_hash, status, version, created_at, updated_at, audit_hash.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Schema matches PRD-0063 EstateDocument shape (subset for MVP); RLS enabled; sha256 NOT NULL.

leaves.statement: Documents stored encrypted-at-rest using AES-256 via tenant-level KMS key; per-document key deferred (ASM-SEC-001).
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Object-store inspection shows ciphertext (not plaintext PDF); ADR records ASM-SEC-001 deferral.

leaves.statement: Allowed MIME types: application/pdf only at MVP; other types rejected with 415.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Upload of .docx returns 415; upload of malformed .pdf returns 400 with error 'invalid PDF'.

leaves.statement: All state-changing operations write a row to audit_events with id, tenant_id, actor_user_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, ts, request_id, prev_audit_hash, audit_hash, payload_json.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: After upload + extraction + calc + scenario operations, audit_events count increases by exactly the expected number; chain validation: each row.prev_audit_hash == previous row.audit_hash.

leaves.statement: audit_events table is append-only via DB trigger that RAISES EXCEPTION on UPDATE or DELETE; minimum retention 7 years (PRD-0104).
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Attempt UPDATE audit_events SET action='X' raises exception; attempt DELETE FROM audit_events WHERE id=... raises exception; retention column or row_lock_until_ts set to now()+7y.

leaves.statement: audit_events.payload_json for AI operations includes promptHash, modelVersion, citations[], guardrailStatus, deterministicValidation per PRD-0180 shape.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: After extraction operation, audit row's payload_json validates against ai-audit-event JSON schema with all 5 required fields populated.

leaves.statement: Document events emitted: document.uploaded, document.viewed, document.extraction_started, document.extraction_completed.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Each event written to audit_events with correct action and resource_id; verified by integration test.

leaves.statement: AI prompt logs anonymize PII at ingestion (PRD-0190) — at MVP we store promptHash only and not the raw prompt unless explicitly enabled by admin debug flag.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: audit_events.payload_json.promptHash present; raw prompt absent unless debug flag set; debug flag check is itself audited.

leaves.statement: Vault RBAC at MVP: advisor read+upload; paraplanner read-only; admin read+audit-export. Client/Attorney/Family roles deferred (CC-001 — Client Portal/Document Creation out of scope).
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Paraplanner POST upload returns 403; advisor returns 201; admin GET /audit-events returns 200 with WORM rows.

capabilities.name: Ester-substitute Extractor (Layout-aware OCR + LLM + Deterministic Calc Validation + Citation Grounding)
capabilities.statement: End-to-end document extraction pipeline producing structured JSON with per-field confidence, citations to source spans, and deterministic-calc validation. Architecture documented as ADR; one extraction flow implemented for sample will/trust document.
capabilities.re_interrogation_rationale: Branch is at 86 validation_readiness which is above 80 threshold; no re-interrogation triggered. Holdout-corpus selection (OQ-INTAKE-005) is the one open item but is implementation-time, not spec-time.

user_journeys.name: Advisor uploads will → reviews extracted JSON with citations + confidence
user_journeys.statement: Advisor uploads sample-will.pdf, system extracts ≥10 structured fields with citations and confidence scores, advisor reviews and confirms.

leaves.statement: Extraction service runs 4-stage pipeline per PRD-0009: (1) pre-process PDF (text extraction + page index), (2) layout-aware OCR fallback for image regions (mocked at MVP via a deterministic stub OCR returning ground-truth for sample), (3) LLM extraction call producing structured JSON, (4) deterministic post-processing applying numerical validation per PRD-0165.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: POST /api/extractions starts pipeline; on completion, /api/extractions/:id returns status='COMPLETED' with structured fields; pipeline logs each stage timestamp.

leaves.statement: Each extracted field has shape {name, value, confidence (0-100), citation: {pageIndex, spanStart, spanEnd, snippet}, deterministicValidation (boolean)}.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Sample-will.pdf extraction produces ≥10 fields each with all 5 properties populated; spans must contain text actually present in the PDF page (verified by reading raw text and substring-checking).

leaves.statement: Deterministic-calc validation: for any numeric field (e.g., dollar amount, percentage, date), the deterministic post-processor recomputes from raw span text and accepts only if within 0.0001 tolerance vs LLM-proposed value.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Field 'principalAmount: $5,000,000' parsed from snippet '$5,000,000.00' yields deterministicValidation:true; LLM-hallucinated $5,500,000 with snippet $5M yields deterministicValidation:false and field rejected.

leaves.statement: Per-field confidence routing: fields with confidence <85 are flagged status='REVIEW_NEEDED' (PRD-0078); MVP renders flag in UI but does not implement HITL queue.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Test fixture with 1 field at 80% confidence shows status='REVIEW_NEEDED'; field at 90% shows status='ACCEPTED'.

leaves.statement: Threshold: aggregate field-level accuracy on holdout ≥95% (PRD-0079); aggregate hallucination rate <0.5% (looser PRD-0124 figure adopted per ANS-0061).
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Holdout test on 5 public-corpus wills: (correct fields / total fields) ≥ 0.95; (fabricated fields / total fields) < 0.005.

leaves.statement: extractions table: id, tenant_id, document_id, status, fields_json, confidence_overall, started_at, completed_at, model_version, prompt_hash.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Schema present with RLS; one row per extraction; fields_json validates against extraction-result JSON schema.

leaves.statement: Every extracted field must have a citation pointing to a (pageIndex, spanStart, spanEnd) tuple in the source PDF; citation field must be non-null at the schema level.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: JSON-schema validation rejects extraction result missing citation; integration test passes citation snippet through PDF.text(pageIndex)[spanStart:spanEnd] and asserts equality.

leaves.statement: Extraction end-to-end p95 latency <60s on a 5-page sample will (relaxed from PRD-0200 <20s; ASM-EXTRACT-LATENCY-001).
leaves.acceptance_criteria: 20 sequential extraction runs measured; p95 < 60s.

leaves.statement: Field-level extraction accuracy ≥95% on public-corpus holdout (5 sample wills/trusts).
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Holdout evaluation script reports accuracy ≥0.95.

leaves.statement: Hallucination rate <0.5% on holdout (fields fabricated where source span doesn't support).
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Holdout evaluation script categorizes each field as supported/fabricated; fabricated/total < 0.005.

leaves.statement: Calculation determinism on numeric fields: 100% match against deterministic recompute within 0.0001 tolerance.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: All numeric fields in holdout have deterministicValidation=true OR are flagged REVIEW_NEEDED; no silently-accepted mismatches.

leaves.statement: ADR-001 LOCKED: Deterministic-calc layer architecturally separated from LLM. LLM extracts and narrates; deterministic engine validates and computes. Refusal rule: any leaf using LLM for tax/estate calc without deterministic fallback is rejected.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: ADR-001 file exists in 05_artifacts/adrs/; references PRD-0072, PRD-0124, PRD-0165, FACT-EXTRACT-COMPUTE-NARRATE-001; Hawkeye check that no leaf violates rule.

leaves.statement: ADR-002: extraction memory at MVP is session-scoped only (no per-household long-term embeddings; PRD-0173 long-term memory deferred).
leaves.acceptance_criteria: ADR-002 file exists; deferred items recorded.

leaves.statement: Every extraction-review UI screen renders disclaimer: 'This is not legal or tax advice; consult your licensed professional.' (PRD-0182).
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Playwright test asserts disclaimer text present on extraction-review and estate-tax and scenario screens.

capabilities.name: Federal Estate Tax Baseline Calc under OBBBA $15M Permanent (One Scenario)
capabilities.statement: Deterministic engine computing federal estate tax baseline for ONE scenario (Ellison $20M household) under OBBBA $15M permanent exemption with simplified 40% top rate. Validated against golden dataset.

user_journeys.name: Advisor opens Estate-Tax view → sees $20M baseline calc
user_journeys.statement: Advisor on Ellison Household opens /households/:id/estate-tax view; deterministic engine runs and renders exemption / taxable / tax / net-to-heirs.

leaves.statement: Function computeFederalEstateTax(totalAssetsUSD, exemptionUSD, topRate) returns {exemption, taxableEstate=max(0, total-exemption), federalTax=taxableEstate*topRate, netToHeirs=total-federalTax}.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: computeFederalEstateTax(20_000_000, 15_000_000, 0.40) === {exemption:15000000, taxableEstate:5000000, federalTax:2000000, netToHeirs:18000000}; pure function (no I/O); deterministic on 1000 repeats.

leaves.statement: OBBBA 2026 individual exemption = $15,000,000 (FACT-OBBBA-001); top federal estate-tax rate = 40%; bracket simplified to flat top rate per ASM-CALC-001.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Constants module exports OBBBA_2026_INDIVIDUAL_EXEMPTION_USD=15_000_000 and FEDERAL_ESTATE_TAX_TOP_RATE=0.40; tax-domain juror sign-off recorded in ADR.

leaves.statement: Recompute tolerance: result must match golden-dataset entry within 0.0001 absolute tolerance (PRD-0165).
leaves.acceptance_criteria: abs(computed - golden) < 0.0001 for every golden case.

leaves.statement: Golden dataset 'OBBBA-15M-baseline' includes ≥3 cases: total=20M→tax=2M; total=15M→tax=0; total=10M→tax=0.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: JSON file 05_artifacts/golden-datasets/estate-tax-obbba.json contains ≥3 cases with explicit expected outputs and OBBBA citation.

leaves.statement: POST /api/households/:id/estate-tax-baseline triggers calc + writes calc_runs row + returns full result JSON; idempotent (same input yields same output, same calc_run_id reused if no input changes).
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Two consecutive calls return same calc_run_id; result includes deterministicValidation:true + citation to RULE-CALC-001 + disclaimer.

leaves.statement: calc_runs table: id, tenant_id, household_id, scenario_type ('FEDERAL_ESTATE_TAX_BASELINE'), inputs_json, outputs_json, deterministic_validation, created_at, model_version='deterministic-v1.0'.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Schema present with RLS; outputs_json validates against estate-tax-result JSON schema.

leaves.statement: Event 'calc.estate_tax_baseline.completed' written to audit_events with payload {household_id, exemption, federalTax, deterministicValidation, citations:['FACT-OBBBA-001']}.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Audit row present after each calc run; payload validates.

leaves.statement: Estate-tax baseline calc p95 latency <500ms.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: 100 sequential calc runs; p95 < 500ms.

leaves.statement: Calc determinism: 100% — same input always yields same output across 1000 iterations and across server restarts.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Determinism test runs 1000 iterations and asserts identical bytes-of-output.

capabilities.name: Scenario Builder — GRAT vs Do-Nothing for $20M Household (One Scenario)
capabilities.statement: Side-by-side scenario comparison of GRAT vs do-nothing strategies for $20M household under OBBBA. Section 7520 rate slider; deterministic projection of estate-tax delta; GRAT remainder validated within 0.0001 tolerance.

user_journeys.name: Advisor opens scenario view → side-by-side GRAT vs do-nothing
user_journeys.statement: Advisor on Ellison Household selects 'Run Scenario: GRAT vs do-nothing'; UI renders two columns with projection and tax delta; Section 7520 rate slider re-runs calc.

leaves.statement: Scenario inputs default to PRD-0157 sample defaults: assetGrowthPct=7%, gratTermYears=5, section7520RatePct=4.8%, gratFundingUSD=$5,000,000; advisor can override via slider/input.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: UI renders defaults; user changes slider 4.8→6.0%, recomputed result reflects new 7520 rate within <1.5s.

leaves.statement: Scenario uses OBBBA $15M exemption (not PRD-0157's repealed $7M); explicit obbba_correction_applied:true marker.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Output JSON includes 'obbba_correction_applied: true' and reference to FACT-OBBBA-001; PRD-0157 repealed assumption documented as superseded.

leaves.statement: Function projectGratVsDoNothing(inputs) returns {pathA_doNothing: {projectedAssetsAtTermEnd, projectedFederalEstateTax, netToHeirs}, pathB_grat: {gratRemainderToBeneficiary, projectedAssetsAtTermEnd, projectedFederalEstateTax, netToHeirs}, taxDelta}.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Pure function on synthetic inputs returns finite numbers; pathA tax delta vs pathB delta computed deterministically.

leaves.statement: GRAT remainder computed using IRS Section 7520 annuity-formula: remainder = funded - PV(annuity@7520Rate, term); validated within 0.0001 vs golden case (PRD-0207).
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Golden case: $5M funded, 5y term, 4.8% 7520 rate, qualified annuity at 100% → remainder ≈ expected golden value within 0.0001.

leaves.statement: Golden dataset 'GRAT-OBBBA-20M' includes ≥3 cases at different 7520 rates (3.0%, 4.8%, 6.0%) and term lengths (3, 5, 10 years).
leaves.acceptance_criteria: JSON file 05_artifacts/golden-datasets/grat-scenario.json contains ≥3 cases with expected remainder + projected estate tax.

leaves.statement: POST /api/households/:id/scenarios/grat-vs-do-nothing accepts inputs, returns projection JSON, writes calc_runs + audit event.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Endpoint returns 200 with full projection; calc_runs row exists; audit event 'scenario.grat_vs_donothing.completed' present.

leaves.statement: Audit event 'scenario.grat_vs_donothing.completed' carries deterministicValidation:true + citations to FACT-OBBBA-001 and PRD-0207.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Verified by integration test reading audit_events.payload_json.

leaves.statement: Slider re-render p95 latency <1.5s after input change.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Playwright timing test: change slider, await UI update; p95 over 20 changes <1.5s.

leaves.statement: Scenario determinism: same inputs yield same outputs across 1000 iterations.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Determinism test passes.

leaves.statement: Strategies SLAT, ILIT, CLAT, Dynasty, Revocable, QPRT, IDGT, CRT/CRUT explicitly out-of-scope per CC-001; only GRAT-vs-do-nothing implemented at MVP.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Scenario type enum contains only 'GRAT_VS_DO_NOTHING'; ADR records deferral list with rollback path.

capabilities.name: Minimal Advisor Walkable UI
capabilities.statement: Web UI for advisor demo flow: login → dashboard → household list → household detail → document upload → extraction review → estate-tax view → scenario view. Desktop-first, WCAG 2.1 AA, AI-Trust heuristics applied.

user_journeys.name: Advisor walkable demo flow end-to-end
user_journeys.statement: Sarah-archetype advisor logs in, navigates dashboard → household → upload → extraction review → estate-tax → scenario; every screen Playwright-evidenced with WCAG-AA pass.

leaves.statement: /login page renders form with email + password + 'Sign In' button; on submit calls POST /api/auth/login; on success navigates to /mfa-verify; on failure renders inline error.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Playwright: navigate, fill, click; failure path shows error message; success path navigates to /mfa-verify.

leaves.statement: /mfa-verify page renders 6-digit code input + 'Verify' button; on success navigates to /dashboard.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Playwright completes MFA flow with seed code 123456 and lands on /dashboard.

leaves.statement: /dashboard renders household list table with name + total assets; each row links to /households/:id.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Playwright: as tenant A, table has 3 rows; as tenant B, 2 rows; clicking Ellison row navigates to detail.

leaves.statement: /households/:id renders persons table, entities table (with Trust types badges), assets table, documents list, and 4 action buttons: Upload Document, View Estate Tax, Run Scenario, View Audit.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Playwright: page shows expected counts of persons/entities/assets per seed; action buttons navigate to correct routes.

leaves.statement: Upload modal supports drag-drop and click-to-browse for PDF; shows progress bar; on success renders document row in list and navigates to extraction-review.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Playwright: setInputFiles with sample-will.pdf; await progress 100%; document appears in list.

leaves.statement: Extraction-review page renders fields table with columns: name, value, confidence (color-coded), citation snippet, deterministicValidation badge. Fields with REVIEW_NEEDED status visually distinguished.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Playwright: page shows ≥10 field rows; rows with confidence <85 have yellow 'review needed' badge; clicking citation snippet opens PDF at correct page (mock viewer at MVP).

leaves.statement: Every extraction-review, estate-tax, and scenario screen renders the disclaimer 'This is not legal or tax advice; consult your licensed professional.' in a footer banner.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Playwright getByText() finds disclaimer on each screen.

leaves.statement: /households/:id/estate-tax renders 4-tile summary (Total Assets, Exemption $15M (OBBBA), Taxable Estate, Federal Tax) plus a 'View Calculation Trace' expandable section with deterministic calc trace + citations + disclaimer.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Playwright: tiles show {$20M, $15M, $5M, $2M}; expand trace shows formula with citations to FACT-OBBBA-001 and PRD-0207.

leaves.statement: /households/:id/scenarios/grat-vs-do-nothing renders side-by-side cards (Path A: Do Nothing, Path B: Fund GRAT) with key metrics, Section 7520 rate slider, term-years dropdown, asset-growth slider, and a 'Tax Delta' headline.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Playwright: change 7520 slider 4.8→6.0; both cards re-render with new numbers; tax delta updates; assertion holds.

leaves.statement: Every advisor screen passes WCAG 2.1 AA via axe-core; zero AA violations.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Playwright + axe-core on each route reports 0 AA violations.

leaves.statement: Keyboard navigation works on all interactive elements (Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter, Esc); focus indicator visible.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Playwright keyboard-only walkthrough completes the entire demo flow without mouse.

leaves.statement: Design tokens: deep navy #0A2540 + soft sage #E8F0E8 (PRD-0126); Tailwind+Radix primitives.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: tailwind.config.js exports the two tokens; component spot-check confirms usage.

leaves.statement: AI-Trust heuristics applied: Transparency (every AI-derived value shows confidence + citation), Control (advisor can override field values; not implemented but UI affordance present), Emotional Safety (no aggressive 'sell more' nudges), Consistency (same disclaimer text everywhere) per PRD-0128.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Manual review checklist signed off; spot-check on extraction and scenario screens confirms.

leaves.statement: Brand substitution: no use of 'Wealth.com', 'Ester®', 'Heritage Map', 'Family Office Suite™', 'EstateFlow™', 'Plan for Every Tomorrow™'. Working title 'EstateCompass' / internal 'Sage'.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: grep across UI source for forbidden terms returns 0 matches; only 'EstateCompass'/'Sage' used.

leaves.statement: Brand validator runs as a runtime gate per generated-meta-skill-contract; CI failure if forbidden term appears.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: CI script scans /app/web /app/api /app/ai for forbidden brand terms; fails build if any found.

capabilities.name: Two Integration Adapter Mocks (Plaid-shape + Schwab-shape)
capabilities.statement: Contract-tested mock adapters for Plaid (account aggregation) and Schwab (custodial holdings). Demonstrates anti-corruption layer pattern; no real partner endpoints called.

user_journeys.name: Demo invokes Plaid-shape and Schwab-shape adapters; verified by contract tests
user_journeys.statement: Demo orchestrator calls adapter.fetchAccounts(Plaid-shape) and adapter.fetchHoldings(Schwab-shape); responses validated against published partner JSON Schemas.

leaves.statement: Plaid-shape adapter: function fetchAccounts({tenantId, householdId, accessToken}) returns array of account objects matching Plaid /accounts/get response shape (subset: account_id, name, type, subtype, balances:{available, current}).
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Calling adapter returns recorded golden response (synthetic accounts only) shaped to Plaid contract; never makes outbound HTTP to plaid.com.

leaves.statement: Plaid adapter base URL is config-bound to a mock server URL; production-URL safeguard: any URL containing 'plaid.com' rejected at runtime.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Setting config to https://api.plaid.com fails startup with explicit error; setting to http://mock-plaid:8081 succeeds.

leaves.statement: Recorded golden response file 05_artifacts/integration-mocks/plaid-accounts-get.golden.json contains synthetic Ellison household accounts.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: File present; JSON-schema validation against published Plaid OpenAPI for /accounts/get passes.

leaves.statement: Schwab-shape adapter: function fetchHoldings({tenantId, householdId, accountToken}) returns array of holding objects shaped to Schwab Custodial API (account, securities[]:{symbol, quantity, marketValue, costBasis}).
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Calling adapter returns recorded golden response (synthetic holdings); contract test green.

leaves.statement: Schwab adapter base URL is config-bound to mock; any 'schwab.com' or 'schwabapi.com' URL rejected at runtime.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Production URL fails startup; mock URL succeeds.

leaves.statement: Recorded golden response file 05_artifacts/integration-mocks/schwab-holdings.golden.json contains synthetic Ellison custodial holdings.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: File present; JSON-schema validation passes.

leaves.statement: Tenant-isolation in adapter: passing tenant A token but householdId from tenant B returns 403; adapter call audited.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Forge attempt rejected with 403; audit_events row created.

leaves.statement: Adapter response time (mock): p95 <300ms.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: 100 sequential calls; p95 <300ms.

leaves.statement: Other 17+ PRD partners (Salesforce FSC, Redtail, Wealthbox, Orion, Black Diamond, Addepar, eMoney, MoneyGuidePro, RightCapital, Holistiplan, Fidelity, Pershing, Carta, Coinbase, Anchorage, Zillow, DocuSign, Notarize, M365, Google, Snowflake, Advyzon, Goldman, Arch, Zocks/Jump) explicitly out of MVP per CC-001.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: ADR records deferral list; no leaf cites those partners as in-scope.

cross_cutting_capabilities.name: Cross-cutting Concerns (Compliance, Brand, Anti-Overfit, Operability)
cross_cutting_capabilities.statement: Concerns spanning multiple capabilities: compliance disclaimer/UPL, brand substitution, synthetic-data-only, transfer-test, operability runbook for demo.

leaves.statement: All in-scope leaves comply with SEC Rule 204-2 (5-year minimum records) and FINRA 17a-4(f) WORM via audit_events 7-year retention.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Compliance review confirms audit_events retention=7y, immutable; matches SEC 204-2 and FINRA 17a-4(f).

leaves.statement: GLBA + state privacy posture for synthetic data; ADMT (CCPA/CPRA Jan 1, 2027) flagged as forward risk; NYDFS Part 500 noted but production_surface=false defers control implementation.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Compliance ADR records posture and forward risks.

leaves.statement: Operator runs `docker compose up` from runs/wealth-com/app/ to start demo; `docker compose down -v` to reset; README.md documents both commands.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Fresh checkout: docker compose up succeeds; demo flow accessible at http://localhost:3000; docker compose down -v removes all volumes.

leaves.statement: production_surface=false at MVP (HD-004); release plan + runbook + outage drill deferred.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: ATTR + tailoring profile flags production_surface=false; deferral recorded.

leaves.statement: OpenTelemetry traces enabled on every API route + extraction job + calc + scenario; exported to local OTel collector logged to file.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Trace file shows spans for the demo flow; tenant_id and request_id present.

leaves.statement: Transfer test: a Holistiplan-style mini-app (single-input tax-baseline calc surface) is implemented using factory-reusable scaffolding (no wealth.com nouns leaking); tests pass and Hawkeye anti-overfit auditor signs off.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Mini-app builds + tests green; factory rubric inspection finds 0 wealth.com noun leaks; Hawkeye anti-overfit issues PASS.

leaves.statement: Anti-overfit: no wealth.com noun (Ester, GRAT, Heritage Map, Family Office Suite, EstateFlow) appears in factory-reusable rubrics or templates. They are allowed only in product-specific runs/wealth-com/* artifacts.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Hawkeye Stage-1 runs grep across factory-template directories and factory-rubric files for forbidden tokens; 0 matches.

leaves.statement: Evidence rule: every Must leaf in this decomposition must have ≥1 of {unit, integration, scenario_bdd, holdout, golden_dataset, playwright, contract_api} test record before claim of 'pass'. No documents-only completion.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: Trace matrix shows every M leaf has ≥1 test artifact reference; Hawkeye verifies.

leaves.statement: Trace coverage: ≥95% of in-scope MVP atoms cited by ≥1 leaf; 100% of leaves cite ≥1 atom OR ≥1 answer OR ≥1 explicit ASM.
leaves.acceptance_criteria: intake-validation-report.json reports trace coverage values meeting thresholds.

verdict_rationale: All 8 in-scope capabilities decomposed to atomic, testable leaves. Branch-level completeness and validation_readiness scores all ≥80. Every leaf has parent, owner, AC, validation method, dependencies, risks, and trace targets. OBBBA correction applied to all tax-related leaves. Cross-cutting concerns covered. No re-interrogation required.
